title: Kernelについて、soul.mdもしくはphilosophy.mdを作るべきか？方法としてはobsidianにある情報を抽出して作成する。マルチエージェント・オーケストレーションで点数化して評価せよ
summary_text: 1. [High] Bash 3.2 compatibility is already broken as-is (`declare -A` for `SKILL_PATHS`), so adding the filter does not change that unless you also remove associative arrays or require Bash 4+. 2. [High] Avoid `PHILOSOPHY_WORLDVIEW_SKILLS` as a simple substring match (e.g., `[[ "$SKILL_KEYS[@]" == *"$val"* ]]`); it causes partial matches (`slide` matching `slide-script-prep`), and can skip/overrun keys silently. 3. [Medium] If you parse a comma/space-delimited env value, don’t rely on `${var/..}` expansions for tokenization; split with an explicit loop using `IFS` and trim whitespace to avoid empty/whitespace tokens in the selection set. 4. [Medium] In Bash 3.2, avoid nameref/features like `${!arr[@]}`/`mapfile`-style patterns for filtering; keep selection with straightforward loops and case-safe comparison. 5. [Low] Preserve “all-by-default” behavior with `PHILOSOPHY_WORLDVIEW_SKILLS` unset, and validate tokens against the canonical key set (`slide-script-prep`, `notion-research-documentation`, `notion-meeting-intelligence`, `notion-knowledge-capture`) to avoid silently running nothing (or a typo key).
